verdict_reasoning: LYN is an inner-leaflet-anchored SRC-family kinase (myristoylated, no TM domain). At baseline it localizes to the cytoplasmic face of PM, ER, and Golgi. However, LYN has a documented outer-leaflet pool under non-baseline conditions: (1) cell-death-induced externalization during apoptosis/necroptosis (inner-leaflet proteins are exposed on the outer face via blebbing and PS exposure); (2) cancer-cell ecto-presentation in hematopoietic malignancies, where tumor cells display ecto-LYN as part of their immunogenic surface architecture. These non-baseline ecto-pools meet the contextual threshold for surface accessibility.
predicted_key_uncertainty: The quantitative extent and stability of the cancer-cell and cell-death ecto-LYN pools are not fully characterized in the literature. It is unclear whether the ecto-pool is large enough or stable enough to support therapeutic targeting, or whether it remains transient/minor. The canonical baseline state is unambiguously inner-leaflet-anchored.
